meta.title: MS-FAE-1: Fabric Lakehouse, DirectLake, Semantic Models and Governance

domains.name: SQL Analytics Endpoint and Lakehouse SQL

questions.stem: Which Microsoft Fabric feature provides a single, tenant-wide logical data lake for Fabric workloads?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because OneLake is the storage foundation shared across Fabric workloads. **A** is incorrect because A warehouse is one item type rather than the tenant-wide storage layer. **C** is incorrect because Dataflows Gen2 performs transformations rather than acting as tenant storage. **D** is incorrect because Deployment pipelines promote content between stages rather than storing data. [Microsoft Fabric OneLake](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-overview)

questions.stem: A team wants to expose files stored in Amazon S3 inside Fabric without copying them into OneLake first. Which feature should they use?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because OneLake shortcuts let Fabric reference external storage such as ADLS Gen2 and Amazon S3 without copying data. **A** is incorrect because DirectLake is a semantic model storage mode, not an external-storage link. **C** is incorrect because Deployment pipeline rules manage release behavior, not storage access. **D** is incorrect because A KQL database stores event data and does not virtualize S3 files. [OneLake shortcuts](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-shortcuts)

questions.stem: What is the main difference between a Fabric Lakehouse and a Fabric Data Warehouse?

options.text: Lakehouses support only CSV files while warehouses support Delta tables
Lakehouses are Spark-first over Delta files, whereas warehouses provide a SQL data warehousing experience
Lakehouses do not create a SQL endpoint

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because a Lakehouse centers on Delta files and Spark workflows while a Warehouse is the SQL-centric warehousing experience. **A** is incorrect because Lakehouses are not limited to CSV files. **C** is incorrect because Warehouses do support transactional behavior. **D** is incorrect because Lakehouses automatically include a SQL analytics endpoint. [What is a lakehouse in Microsoft Fabric?](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview)

questions.stem: What is the SQL analytics endpoint of a Fabric Lakehouse primarily used for?

options.text: Running read-only T-SQL queries over lakehouse tables

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because the SQL analytics endpoint offers read-only SQL access over lakehouse Delta tables. **B** is incorrect because Spark notebooks run in the Spark engine, not the SQL endpoint. **C** is incorrect because capacity settings are administered at workspace or tenant level. **D** is incorrect because publishing apps is a Power BI service task. [SQL analytics endpoint for lakehouses](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-sql-analytics-endpoint)

questions.stem: A Fabric pipeline must retry a copy operation automatically after transient failures. Which pipeline capability addresses this requirement most directly?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because retry settings on a pipeline activity handle transient failures without redesigning the flow. **A** is incorrect because workspace roles control access, not retry behavior. **C** is incorrect because DirectLake framing relates to query execution. **D** is incorrect because endorsement labels trust content rather than controlling activity execution. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: Which Fabric item uses Power Query M as its primary transformation language?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because Dataflows Gen2 are Power Query based and use M for low-code transformations. **A** is incorrect because Spark jobs use Spark languages such as Python or Scala. **C** is incorrect because warehouse stored procedures are T-SQL based. **D** is incorrect because Real-Time Dashboards visualize data instead of transforming it. [Dataflows Gen2 in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: A semantic model should keep the logic reusable across many reports and avoid re-creating the same aggregation in every visual. What should the team create?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because measures centralize reusable business logic in the semantic model. **B** is incorrect because visual calculations are scoped to a visual rather than the model. **C** is incorrect because shortcuts expose storage paths rather than model logic. **D** is incorrect because workspace identity is unrelated to analytical calculations. [DAX basics in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-quickstart-learn-dax-basics)

questions.stem: A report must read the newest parquet data directly from OneLake with minimal latency and without importing data into VertiPaq. Which storage mode fits best?
questions.explanation: **C** is correct because DirectLake reads data from OneLake-backed parquet/Delta storage without a scheduled import and without translating every query to the source like classic DirectQuery. **A** is incorrect because Import requires loading data into the in-memory model first. **B** is incorrect because DirectQuery sends queries through to a source rather than reading directly from OneLake files. **D** is incorrect because a live connection targets an external semantic model or Analysis Services instance. [Direct Lake in Power BI and Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/directlake-overview)

questions.stem: Which behavior should an analytics engineer expect when a DirectLake semantic model cannot stay in DirectLake mode for a query?

options.text: Fabric falls back to DirectQuery for supported scenarios
The report silently switches to Import mode permanently
The dataset is deleted and recreated

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because DirectLake has guardrails and can fall back to DirectQuery in some scenarios. **A** is incorrect because guardrail conditions do not always cause immediate failure. **C** is incorrect because Fabric does not permanently convert the model to Import just because of one query path. **D** is incorrect because datasets are not deleted as part of fallback behavior. [Direct Lake in Power BI and Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/directlake-overview)

questions.stem: What is the purpose of endorsement labels such as Promoted and Certified in Fabric and Power BI?

options.text: To indicate trusted, governed content to users
To allocate more capacity units automatically

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because endorsement helps users discover and trust curated content. **A** is incorrect because endorsement is not an encryption feature. **C** is incorrect because it does not change capacity allocation. **D** is incorrect because it complements rather than replaces security models. [Promoted and certified items](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-endorsement)

questions.stem: A semantic model should hide a sensitive salary column from report builders while keeping other columns in the same table available. Which control is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because OLS can hide specific tables or columns from consumers. **A** is incorrect because RLS filters rows, not columns or objects. **C** is incorrect because deployment pipeline rules manage release configuration. **D** is incorrect because V-order is a storage optimization. [Object-level security](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/security/service-admin-object-level-security)

questions.stem: A regional manager should see only rows for their own sales region in every report that uses the same semantic model. Which feature should be implemented?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because RLS filters rows for each user or role at query time. **A** is incorrect because OLS hides objects rather than filtering row sets. **C** is incorrect because query folding is about pushing transformations to a source. **D** is incorrect because shortcut mirroring is not the Fabric security mechanism here. [Row-level security with Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/service-admin-rls)

questions.stem: Which capability promotes content from development to test to production while preserving stage separation in Fabric?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because deployment pipelines are the release-management feature for promoting Fabric and Power BI content. **B** is incorrect because shortcuts virtualize storage paths. **C** is incorrect because Dataflows Gen2 staging is for transformation execution. **D** is incorrect because SQL endpoints query data rather than promote artifacts. [Deployment pipelines in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/cicd/deployment-pipelines/intro-to-deployment-pipelines)

questions.stem: What does the Fabric Capacity Metrics app help an analytics engineer monitor?

options.text: DAX syntax errors inside a single report
Capacity utilization, throttling, and workload consumption

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because the metrics app exposes CU utilization and throttling behavior across Fabric workloads. **A** is incorrect because it is not a report-specific DAX debugger. **C** is incorrect because workspace membership is handled elsewhere. **D** is incorrect because it is broader than SQL endpoint indexing. [Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/enterprise/metrics-app)

questions.stem: A team needs analysts to query curated Delta tables with T-SQL but data engineers still want notebook access to the same data. Which Fabric design best meets both needs?

options.text: Store the data only in an Eventhouse
Use a Lakehouse so Spark notebooks and the SQL analytics endpoint can both access the tables
Use only a Power BI dataflow
Use a Warehouse because notebooks cannot work with Lakehouses

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because a Lakehouse supports Spark notebook workflows and exposes a SQL analytics endpoint for analysts. **A** is incorrect because Eventhouse targets real-time/KQL workloads rather than this dual Spark+SQL need. **C** is incorrect because a dataflow alone is not the shared analytical store. **D** is incorrect because notebooks do work with Lakehouses. [What is a lakehouse in Microsoft Fabric?](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview)

questions.stem: Which Fabric orchestration item is best for sequencing copy, notebook, and stored procedure activities?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because data pipelines orchestrate multi-step movement and transformation workflows. **A** is incorrect because semantic models serve BI queries. **C** is incorrect because DirectLake is a model storage mode. **D** is incorrect because a KQL query set stores KQL queries rather than orchestrating tasks. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: When should an analytics engineer prefer a calculated column over a measure?

options.text: When the value must be evaluated once per row and stored in the model context
When the value should aggregate dynamically by filter context only
When the logic must run only inside a pipeline

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because calculated columns are row-context expressions persisted in the model, whereas measures aggregate dynamically by filter context. **B** is incorrect because dynamic aggregation is the main use case for measures. **C** is incorrect because pipelines are unrelated to DAX column logic. **D** is incorrect because DirectLake does not by itself require calculated columns. [DAX basics in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-quickstart-learn-dax-basics)

questions.stem: What is the primary benefit of incremental refresh in a large Fabric semantic model?

options.text: It disables partitioning to simplify refresh
It refreshes only recent partitions instead of reprocessing all historical data
It forces every visual to use DirectQuery
It replaces the need for a date table

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because incremental refresh reduces refresh cost by processing only defined periods and optionally detecting changes. **A** is incorrect because it relies on partitioning rather than disabling it. **C** is incorrect because it does not force DirectQuery. **D** is incorrect because date dimensions remain useful. [Configure incremental refresh](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/incremental-refresh-overview)

questions.stem: A model contains two relationships from `Sales` to `Date`, one for order date and one for ship date. Which modeling pattern best describes this?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because a single date dimension reused in different relationship roles is a role-playing dimension. **B** is incorrect because a bridge table addresses many-to-many scenarios. **C** is incorrect because SCD describes historical attribute handling. **D** is incorrect because a degenerate dimension is a fact-side key. [Semantic model modes in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: A pipeline loads on-premises SQL Server data into Fabric, but the source is not publicly reachable. What additional component is typically required?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because an on-premises data gateway securely bridges cloud-managed Fabric services to local data sources. **A** is incorrect because OLS secures model objects. **C** is incorrect because DirectLake caching is unrelated to connectivity. **D** is incorrect because shortcut policies do not replace secure gateway connectivity. [Dataflows Gen2 in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: Which statement about OneLake shortcuts is accurate?

options.text: They always duplicate source files into Fabric-managed storage
They provide a metadata link to data in another location
They can be created only for Fabric Warehouses
They require DirectQuery in Power BI

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because shortcuts virtualize access to data in another OneLake location or supported external store without copying the data. **A** is incorrect because copying is not required. **C** is incorrect because they are not limited to Warehouses. **D** is incorrect because they are independent of DirectQuery. [OneLake shortcuts](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-shortcuts)

questions.stem: A DirectLake model is showing poor performance because required columns are not being loaded efficiently into memory. Which Fabric concept is most closely related to how DirectLake reads column segments?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because DirectLake framing governs how the engine loads column data for query execution. **B** is incorrect because snapshot isolation is a database transaction concept. **C** is incorrect because gateway clustering is unrelated. **D** is incorrect because cross-filter direction is a model relationship setting. [Direct Lake in Power BI and Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/directlake-overview)

questions.stem: Which choice best supports governed self-service reuse of curated logic across many reports?

options.text: Embed every calculation separately in each report visual
Store only CSV exports in OneLake

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because a shared semantic model centralizes measures, relationships, and security for reuse. **B** is incorrect because duplicating logic in visuals hurts governance. **C** is incorrect because notebooks alone do not provide governed BI reuse. **D** is incorrect because CSV exports are not a semantic layer. [Semantic model modes in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: A Fabric administrator wants users to discover trusted datasets quickly while preserving strict access controls separately. Which combination is most appropriate?

options.text: Use endorsement for trust signaling and RLS/OLS for access control
Use endorsement instead of security roles
Use workspace roles only and avoid semantic-model security
Use DirectLake fallback rules as a governance control

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because endorsement communicates trust and curation, while RLS and OLS enforce access limits. **B** is incorrect because endorsement does not replace security. **C** is incorrect because workspace roles alone are often too coarse for model data security. **D** is incorrect because DirectLake fallback is a performance/runtime behavior, not access governance. [Promoted and certified items](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-endorsement)

questions.stem: Why might a team choose Dataflows Gen2 instead of a Spark notebook for a simple source-to-target cleansing process?

options.text: Dataflows Gen2 provide a low-code Power Query authoring experience
Dataflows Gen2 are the only way to schedule refreshes
Spark notebooks cannot read files in OneLake

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Dataflows Gen2 are designed for low-code transformation with Power Query. **B** is incorrect because scheduling is not unique to Dataflows. **C** is incorrect because Spark notebooks can read OneLake data. **D** is incorrect because dataflows do not replace semantic models. [Dataflows Gen2 in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: An analyst needs to query the same lakehouse tables with familiar T-SQL syntax but should not modify the underlying data. Which endpoint should the analyst use?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because the lakehouse SQL analytics endpoint is read-only T-SQL access. **A** is incorrect because a warehouse endpoint is a different item and supports broader SQL operations. **C** is incorrect because the monitoring UI is for Spark diagnostics. **D** is incorrect because the metrics app is for capacity monitoring. [SQL analytics endpoint for lakehouses](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-sql-analytics-endpoint)

questions.stem: Which semantic model storage mode typically provides the fastest interactive performance after data is fully loaded into memory?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Import keeps compressed data in-memory for fast interactive queries. **B** is incorrect because DirectQuery depends on source query latency. **C** is incorrect because live connection uses an external model rather than local storage mode. **D** is incorrect because shortcut mode is not a semantic model storage mode. [Semantic model modes in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: Which pipeline design helps route failures to an alternate notification branch without stopping successful downstream paths unnecessarily?

options.text: Conditional paths based on activity outcome

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because conditional branching lets a pipeline react to success or failure outcomes explicitly. **B** is incorrect because endorsement is unrelated to orchestration. **C** is incorrect because OLS secures data objects. **D** is incorrect because role-playing dimensions are modeling constructs. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: A composite modeling design uses both imported dimensions and a live query path for detailed facts. Which semantic-model concept does this describe?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because a composite model combines multiple storage behaviors in one semantic design. **B** is incorrect because shortcut mirroring is not a semantic model pattern. **C** is incorrect because SQL endpoint is a query surface. **D** is incorrect because gateway cluster is infrastructure. [Semantic model modes in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: Which permission scope is generally broader: workspace roles or item-level sharing?

options.text: Item-level sharing is broader in all cases
Workspace roles are broader because they govern access across items in the workspace
Neither can grant access to reports

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because workspace roles apply across the workspace and are therefore typically broader than sharing an individual item. **A** is incorrect because item-level sharing is more targeted. **C** is incorrect because they are not identical controls. **D** is incorrect because both can participate in report access scenarios. [Row-level security with Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/service-admin-rls)

questions.stem: Which Fabric item is most aligned with SQL-centric dimensional warehousing and cross-database style analytical querying?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because the Warehouse item is the SQL-centric warehousing experience in Fabric. **B** is incorrect because notebooks are code authoring tools. **C** is incorrect because Dataflows transform data. **D** is incorrect because dashboards visualize results. [Data Warehousing in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-warehouse/data-warehousing)

questions.stem: Why would an analytics engineer create a calculation group in a semantic model?

options.text: To centralize reusable calculation logic such as time intelligence patterns

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because calculation groups reduce repeated DAX patterns such as YTD and variance calculations. **B** is incorrect because they do not move files. **C** is incorrect because they are not encryption settings. **D** is incorrect because they are unrelated to gateways. [DAX basics in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-quickstart-learn-dax-basics)

questions.stem: A pipeline copies data into a lakehouse and then triggers a notebook that expects the copy output path dynamically. What should the engineer use?

options.text: An activity output or parameter passed to the notebook

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because pipeline parameters and activity outputs let downstream steps consume runtime values. **B** is incorrect because endorsement labels are metadata trust signals. **C** is incorrect because role-playing dimensions are semantic modeling patterns. **D** is incorrect because fallback rules are unrelated. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: Which relationship setting most directly controls whether filters flow one way or in both directions between related tables?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because cross-filter direction controls filter propagation across relationships. **B** is incorrect because incremental refresh manages partitions. **C** is incorrect because framing is a DirectLake execution concept. **D** is incorrect because OLS secures objects rather than relationship behavior. [Semantic model modes in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: If capacity usage is persistently throttled during peak business hours, what is the most appropriate first response?

options.text: Review Capacity Metrics and right-size or reschedule workloads
Convert every model to DirectQuery immediately

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because the first step is to verify throttling and workload pressure, then resize capacity or reschedule heavy jobs based on evidence. **B** is incorrect because security roles do not solve capacity pressure. **C** is incorrect because blanket conversion to DirectQuery may worsen performance. **D** is incorrect because removing SQL endpoints does not address root capacity usage. [Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/enterprise/metrics-app)

questions.stem: Which Fabric control determines whether a user can open and edit content across many items in a workspace?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because workspace roles govern broad permissions such as viewing or editing content in a workspace. **B** is incorrect because V-order is a storage optimization. **C** is incorrect because incremental refresh is a model refresh strategy. **D** is incorrect because framing is a DirectLake runtime concept. [Row-level security with Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/service-admin-rls)

questions.stem: A business user wants a trusted certified model for finance and a less-governed exploratory model for ad hoc work. What should the team do?

options.text: Apply different endorsement states to the two models
Use one model only because endorsement must be uniform
Turn off RLS on the exploratory model

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because endorsement states can distinguish curated content from exploratory assets for users. **B** is incorrect because endorsement does not have to be uniform across items. **C** is incorrect because turning off RLS is a separate security choice. **D** is incorrect because there is no need to leave Fabric. [Promoted and certified items](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-endorsement)

questions.stem: Why would a team use a OneLake shortcut instead of copying data into a new lakehouse?

options.text: To reduce data duplication while keeping a single governed access path
To convert the data into DAX measures automatically
To guarantee Import mode in Power BI

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because shortcuts avoid unnecessary duplication and can simplify access to existing data locations. **B** is incorrect because they do not generate DAX. **C** is incorrect because they do not determine Import mode. **D** is incorrect because source access and permissions still matter. [OneLake shortcuts](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-shortcuts)

questions.stem: Which feature helps monitor historical pipeline runs and diagnose which activity failed?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because run history shows execution outcomes and activity details for troubleshooting. **B** is incorrect because shortcuts virtualize storage. **C** is incorrect because calculation groups are DAX metadata. **D** is incorrect because OLS is a security feature. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: Which DAX artifact returns a value based on the current filter context rather than storing one result per row?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because measures are evaluated at query time in filter context. **B** is incorrect because calculated columns are stored row-level expressions. **C** is incorrect because shortcuts and gateways are unrelated. **D** is incorrect because not applicable. [DAX basics in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-quickstart-learn-dax-basics)

questions.stem: When should item-level sharing be preferred over granting a broad workspace role?

options.text: When access should be limited to a specific report or model
When users need full administration rights

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because item-level sharing is more targeted when broad workspace access is unnecessary. **B** is incorrect because it is not for admin rights. **C** is incorrect because capacity sizing is separate. **D** is incorrect because VACUUM is a data maintenance command. [Row-level security with Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/service-admin-rls)

questions.stem: A semantic model uses incremental refresh with change detection. What additional benefit does change detection provide?

options.text: It can avoid refreshing partitions whose tracked change column has not changed
It replaces the date range policy

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because change detection can skip processing partitions with no detected updates. **B** is incorrect because it does not disable pruning. **C** is incorrect because it complements rather than replaces the range policy. **D** is incorrect because it is unrelated to DirectLake mode. [Configure incremental refresh](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/incremental-refresh-overview)

questions.stem: Which Fabric feature is most appropriate for low-code ingestion from many SaaS and database sources into a repeatable workflow?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Copy activity is the Fabric ingestion primitive for many connectors. **B** is incorrect because OLS secures objects. **C** is incorrect because role-playing dimensions are model design. **D** is incorrect because fallback is runtime behavior. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: A DirectLake model shows acceptable report latency but high memory pressure. Which optimization is most likely relevant before rewriting the model?

options.text: Review framing behavior and model design to reduce unnecessary columns

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because DirectLake memory usage depends on what data is framed and loaded; narrowing model design can help. **B** is incorrect because workspace roles do not affect memory pressure materially. **C** is incorrect because CSV exports worsen governance and do not target the issue. **D** is incorrect because endorsement is not a security or performance substitute. [Direct Lake in Power BI and Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/directlake-overview)

questions.stem: Which statement about lakehouse maintenance is accurate in Fabric?

options.text: Engineers can run optimization and cleanup tasks such as `OPTIMIZE` and `VACUUM` on Delta tables
Lakehouses do not support table maintenance
Only the Capacity Metrics app can manage Delta files
Maintenance requires converting the lakehouse to a warehouse

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Fabric lakehouses support Delta maintenance operations. **B** is incorrect because lakehouses do support maintenance. **C** is incorrect because the metrics app monitors capacity rather than maintaining tables. **D** is incorrect because conversion is unnecessary. [What is a lakehouse in Microsoft Fabric?](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview)

questions.stem: Which outcome is most likely if a semantic model is not governed and every report duplicates its own business logic?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because duplicated logic commonly causes inconsistent numbers and harder governance. **B** is incorrect because it does not automatically save capacity. **C** is incorrect because security reuse becomes harder, not stronger. **D** is incorrect because deployment is usually more complex. [Semantic model modes in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: Which storage mode is typically chosen when the source must always be queried live and data should not be imported into the model cache?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because DirectQuery keeps queries against the source system at runtime. **B** is incorrect because Import loads data into the model. **C** is incorrect because DirectLake is specific to OneLake-backed Fabric/Power BI scenarios. **D** is incorrect because role-playing is unrelated. [Semantic model modes in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: A user has access to a workspace but still cannot see a secured table field in a report built from a semantic model. What is the most likely explanation?

options.text: Object-level security hides the field even though workspace access exists
Incremental refresh removed the column permanently
The Capacity Metrics app blocks access

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because workspace access does not override semantic-model OLS restrictions. **B** is incorrect because shortcut latency is unrelated to field visibility. **C** is incorrect because incremental refresh does not permanently remove model columns this way. **D** is incorrect because the metrics app does not enforce report security. [Object-level security](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/security/service-admin-object-level-security)

questions.stem: Why would an engineer add email or alerting steps after critical pipeline branches?

options.text: To make operational failures visible to the support team quickly

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because notifications reduce mean time to awareness when pipelines fail. **B** is incorrect because they do not speed DAX. **C** is incorrect because they do not create shortcuts. **D** is incorrect because they do not certify models. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: Which Fabric workload is most directly associated with notebooks and Spark-based engineering over Delta data?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Data Engineering in Fabric is the Spark-centric workload for notebook-based work over Delta data. **B** is incorrect because Power BI publishing and endorsement are BI governance tasks. **C** is incorrect because Capacity Metrics is for monitoring. **D** is incorrect because not applicable. [What is a lakehouse in Microsoft Fabric?](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview)

questions.stem: A finance report requires both row filtering by region and hiding a sensitive compensation column. Which TWO controls are needed? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because RLS filters which rows each user can see and OLS hides specific columns or tables; together they address both requirements. **C** is incorrect because endorsement communicates trust but does not enforce row or column restrictions. **D** is incorrect because deployment pipelines manage release stages rather than report data entitlements. [Object-level security](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/security/service-admin-object-level-security)

questions.stem: Which TWO Fabric capabilities best support a medallion-style analytics workflow that needs ingestion orchestration and low-code cleansing? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because data pipelines orchestrate movement and sequencing, while Dataflows Gen2 provide low-code transformation for cleansing steps. **C** is incorrect because endorsement is governance metadata, not ETL execution. **D** is incorrect because the Capacity Metrics app monitors workloads rather than implementing transformations. [Dataflows Gen2 in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about DirectLake are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: It can read OneLake-backed data without a full import
It may fall back to DirectQuery in some scenarios
It always requires a gateway to query OneLake files
It is identical to classic Import mode

questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because DirectLake reads directly from OneLake-backed storage and may fall back to DirectQuery depending on constraints or unsupported paths. **C** is incorrect because a gateway is not inherently required just to read OneLake-backed data in Fabric. **D** is incorrect because DirectLake is distinct from classic Import mode even though both can deliver fast queries. [Direct Lake in Power BI and Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/directlake-overview)

questions.stem: Which TWO actions most improve discoverability and trust of enterprise-curated analytics assets? (Select TWO)

options.text: Force every report to use private dataflows

questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because a shared semantic model plus Certified endorsement helps users find and trust governed content. **C** is incorrect because disabling RLS weakens security rather than improving governed trust. **D** is incorrect because private isolated dataflows reduce reuse and discoverability. [Promoted and certified items](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-endorsement)

questions.stem: Why would an analytics engineer choose a Warehouse instead of a Lakehouse for a team of SQL analysts?

options.text: The Warehouse provides a SQL-first experience for dimensional analytics
Warehouses are the only items that can store data in Fabric
Lakehouses cannot be queried with SQL at all

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because the Warehouse is optimized for SQL-centric warehousing patterns. **B** is incorrect because many Fabric items store data. **C** is incorrect because Lakehouses do support SQL querying. **D** is incorrect because governance features still apply. [Data Warehousing in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-warehouse/data-warehousing)

questions.stem: A semantic model contains reusable business calculations that should appear consistently across many reports. Where should those calculations live?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because measures in the model provide centralized reusable logic. **B** is incorrect because visual-only logic duplicates work. **C** is incorrect because shortcuts expose storage. **D** is incorrect because workspace roles are security controls. [DAX basics in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-quickstart-learn-dax-basics)

questions.stem: Which Fabric feature is best for low-code reshaping of source data before it lands in curated analytical assets?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Dataflows Gen2 provide low-code transformation using Power Query. **B** is incorrect because the other choices do not transform source data. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Dataflows Gen2 in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: What is the main benefit of publishing reports against a shared semantic model rather than separate private models?

options.text: Guaranteed lower capacity usage in every case
It eliminates the need for relationships

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because shared semantic models improve consistency and governance. **B** is incorrect because capacity impact depends on design. **C** is incorrect because security still matters. **D** is incorrect because relationships are still important. [Semantic model modes in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: Why should an engineer investigate guardrail-triggered DirectLake fallbacks?

options.text: Fallbacks can change performance characteristics and source-query behavior

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because when a query falls back from DirectLake it may perform differently and depend on DirectQuery behavior. **B** is incorrect because fallbacks do not delete models, guarantee improvement, or disable RLS. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Direct Lake in Power BI and Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/directlake-overview)

questions.stem: Which approach best balances trust signaling and access control for a curated finance model?

options.text: Certify the model and apply RLS/OLS as needed
Certify the model and remove all security filters
Rely only on workspace roles and avoid the semantic model
Use DirectLake fallback as the main control

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because endorsement communicates trust while RLS/OLS enforce access boundaries. **B** is incorrect because certification does not replace security. **C** is incorrect because workspace roles alone may be too coarse. **D** is incorrect because fallback is not a governance control. [Promoted and certified items](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-endorsement)
